MINI MINUTES: No appearances, tentative ruling adopted as follows: DEFENDANT BALWANTSINH THAKOR, SHREE JALARAM HOTEL LP MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Or In The Alternative Nonstatutory Motion To Dismiss (Re Plaintiffs Second Amended Complaint) is continued to March 28, 2018. Parties to provide supplemental briefs including authority to address the following issues raised in opposition: Plaintiffs are suing for the right to return to the Bristol Hotel (Opposition 23:23-24). Plaintiffs are not splitting a cause of action because Bristol 4 brought a specific performance claim (equity) and the present case is for international tort of fraud. (Opposition 16: 24-28). Specifically, the supplemental brief shall cite authorities in support of (in opposition to) Plaintiffs' argument)that bar on issue splitting regarding "the right to return" is not applicable because the earlier action was for equitable relief based on breach of contract theory while the later action sounds in fraud. Supplemental briefs not to exceed 3 pages and to be filed no later than March 27, 2018 by 8:00 a.m. Courtesy copies to be delivered to department 501 no later than 8:30 a.m. on March 27, 2018. Not argued. Both sides agree Plaintiff shall file their amendment no later than October 31, 2017. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

DEFENDANTS BALWANTSINH THAKOR and SHREE JALARAM HOTEL LP'S DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT is sustained with leave to amend. As to the first cause of action Plaintiff shall choose and allege only one of the legal remedies available, i.e. either "affirm the contract and sue for fraud" or "rescission." Conclusory allegations currently made fail to meet the standard. Order is signed in open court. Judge: Ronald E. Quidachay; Clerk: J. Bertelsen; Not Reported (D501)
